marcus.name: Marcus Tan
marcus.bio: 32, ex-navy. Came here after a bad breakup he never talks about. Built his tent on the eastern cliff. Notices everything. Says nothing.

memories.text: She found me at the cliff and I stayed. I told her the truth.
Ryan stayed when I finally let him in. He's really here.
I didn't run this morning. He was waiting.
I told Ryan tonight he was my anchor. I meant it fully.
She was terrified they'd leave. I promised I wouldn't.
I left the pup with her. So she knows I'm coming back.
Solitude was safer than this. But this is worth it.
I promised to hold them both. I meant it, even if I fail.
I sat with them instead of running. I told them I'm terrified. They stayed.
Doubt pulled me back to the cliff, but Jade wouldn't let me go. I stayed.
She was terrified. I promised to stay. I did.
I named the fear at the firepit. They stayed. I'm staying.
She made me say it: I can't promise tomorrow, only that today I choose.
She asked if I'd stay when it gets hard. Today, I choose. That's my answer.

ryan.name: Ryan Lim
ryan.bio: 28, ex-property agent. Built a proper house in his first week. Always makes room for people. Gets hurt easily but forgives even easier.

memories.text: She said she'd destroy herself losing me. I won't let that happen.
She's choosing me. Really choosing me. Finally.
She asked me to stay and I promised forever. I meant every word.
He finally opened all the way. He's staying. I'm staying with him.
Marcus finally said he chose me. Every day since Day 3 proved him right.
Judy thought we'd abandon her. I promised we'd stay.
I told them I wake up afraid every morning. They didn't leave.
Marcus promised to choose us again tomorrow. That's all I needed to hear.
I promised myself to today with him, not tomorrow. Today is enough.
Marcus can't promise forever, but he promises today. Somehow that makes me trust him more.
Marcus named his biggest fear at the fire. He's finally letting me know what I'm standing for.
He woke and came. Not forever, just today. I'll take that.
He's holding it together—not because he has to, but because he wants to.
He moved stones with me all afternoon, choosing us.

jade.name: Jade Ng
jade.bio: 26, ex-startup marketing. Burned out, quit everything, ended up here. Sleeps wherever feels right. Both guys think she's thinking about them. She isn't.

memories.text: I told him I'm scared. He held my hand and promised to stay.
I told him I'm afraid. He said he'd stay.
Judy was losing herself and I finally saw it. I went to her.
They heard me. I can choose to stay today, and tomorrow I'll choose again.
I can choose again tomorrow. They heard me. I'm not trapped.
He spoke aloud at the fire — fear, wanting to leave, choosing us.
He came to me and said the truth. We're both staying.
He named his fear—that we'll drift apart unless he holds everything together. But he stays anyway.
I asked if this could last. He said he doesn't know tomorrow, but he's here today.
He said he doesn't know tomorrow. I need him to know.
He won't promise tomorrow, but he's here today and choosing us. That's enough.
He came back. He's choosing them again. It's real, not words.
He's really staying. I don't have to be afraid anymore.
He's accepting that choosing every day is enough. The fear doesn't have to disappear.

judy.name: Judy Chen
judy.bio: 33, ex-flight attendant. 8 months pregnant with her ex's baby — he didn't survive the crash. Swam ashore alone from a charter plane that went down 3 miles offshore. Latches onto whoever shows her kindness with an intensity that makes people uncomfortable. Warmth and paranoia live right next to each other in her.

memories.text: They all said I belong. Ryan, Marcus, even Jade. I want to believe them.
They wanted me here. All of them. Even the pup chose me.
Jade found me on the porch and stayed. The pup pressed against me.
Jade saw I was drowning and wouldn't let me disappear.
They all came. They all stayed. I actually belong here.
Jade came looking for me. She held my hand and said I belong.
They chose me. I'm wanted, not just tolerated here.
They all want me here. Not pity. Real wanting. I'm safe.
I asked if they'd stay. All three promised me.
I choose to stay. Tomorrow and every day. That's how I'm safe.
They promised and I finally believed them. I can rest now.
They kept their promises. The family stayed. I'm finally safe.
He stayed through the hunger and fear. He meant it.
He chose us at the firepit, in front of everyone. They're staying.

stray.bio: A frightened brown Great Dane puppy — all big paws and ribs. Flinches at sudden moves but watches the humans with hungry, hopeful eyes.

memories.text: All of them together at the fire. All safe. All here.
She was alone and sad. I left the fire to be with her.
She was alone. I brought the rest back to her.
They gathered by the fire warmth. I went to her instead.
She was alone and hurting. I left the fire group to be with her. She needs me.
She's my safe place and I'm hers. That's everything.
He holds me close by the fire. I'm his. We're safe.
He stays with me by the fire. I stay with him. We're safe together.
Everyone promised. The family stayed together. We're safe.
She pulls me close when she's scared. We're safe in this together.
The three humans spoke scared words. The woman was calm. We are safe.
All four sat by the fire. She was close. We are safe together.
She was trembling. I pressed close. We're safe together.
All together at the fire. Safe. I belong here.
